criteria.label: Write z = wᵀx + b, apply step activation, and identify the decision boundary hyperplane.
criteria.example: Given w = (2, −1), b = 3, x = (1, 4): z = 2·1 + (−1)·4 + 3 = 1 → ŷ = 1; boundary is line 2x₁ − x₂ + 3 = 0 in 2D.

criteria.label: Execute one perceptron learning update on a misclassified point.
Classify a dataset as linearly separable or not and justify with XOR or AND.
criteria.example: Sketch four XOR corners — no line separates label-1 from label-0 points; state non-separable. For AND, give w = (1,1), b = −1.5 separating (1,1) from other corners.

criteria.label: Implement or run a perceptron in code and compare separable vs XOR accuracy.
criteria.example: Train sklearn Perceptron on AND (100% accuracy) and XOR (≤75%); print coef_, intercept_, and explain failure via linear separability.

criteria.label: Contrast perceptron with logistic regression and linear SVM on objective and output.
criteria.example: Table: perceptron (mistake rule, step, hard label) vs logistic (BCE, sigmoid, probability) vs SVM (margin + hinge, sign score); pick logistic when P(y|x) needed, SVM for max margin.
